_note: Genuine Abū ʿAmr al-Baṣrī (via al-Dūrī) farsh variants where Ḥafṣ and Warsh agree but Abū ʿAmr reads differently. idx = Ḥafṣ word index in the tokenised verse. Arabic forms come from the KFGQPC Dūrī ʿan Abī ʿAmr mushaf; farsh classification per Ibn al-Jazarī, al-Nashr fī al-qirāʾāt al-ʿashr.
cite: Dūrī ʿan Abī ʿAmr (KFGQPC mushaf); farsh per Ibn al-Jazarī, al-Nashr fī al-qirāʾāt al-ʿashr.

entries.shared: he may pasture and enjoy himself (yartaʿ — jussive, of Joseph)
entries.abuamr: we may pasture and enjoy ourselves (nartaʿ — the brothers, 1st-person plural)
these two (hādhayni — accusative dual, the case grammar expects after inna)
then a pledge (ruhun — an alternative plural of rahn)

entries.shared: striving to frustrate Our signs (muʿājizīn — form III)
entries.abuamr: seeking to thwart Our signs (muʿajjizīn — form II; sense is close)

entries.shared: no intercession will be accepted from it (yuqbalu — masculine passive)
entries.abuamr: no intercession will be accepted from it (tuqbalu — feminine passive, agreeing with shafāʿa)

entries.shared: that He should cause the shore to swallow you (yakhsifa — He)
entries.abuamr: that We should cause the shore to swallow you (nakhsifa — We)
or that We send against you (nursila — We)
